summary: Matched and linked the Taru Cannon item unit by moving previously external item data into src/melee/it/items/ittarucann.c, adding its ItemStateTable, applying small code-generation fixes in two near-matching functions, relabeling related constants as local symbols, and changing configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for ittarucann.c. The decomp.dev bot reported 5 new GALE01 matches: ittarucann .data, .rodata, .sdata2, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned, and it_802962E0.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/ittarucann.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Replaced extern declarations for it_803B8610 and it_803B8634 with in-unit const definitions: a lbColl_80008D30_arg1 initializer and Vec3 { 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f }.

Added ItemStateTable it_803F63C0[] with ten Taru Cannon state entries referencing itTarucann_UnkMotion0/1/2/6/7/8/9 Anim, Phys, and Coll callbacks, including NULL for one collision callback and casts to HSD_GObjEvent for UnkMotion7_Phys.
Added includes for types.h and baselib/forward.h in ittarucann.c.
Added static f32 fake1() and fake2() helpers returning -1.0f and 1.3089969f respectively; the diff gives no explanatory comment for these helpers.
Adjusted it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned so the 1.3089969f addend is first assigned through a temporary variable before adding temp, addressing the nearby @todo float regswap note.
Rewrote an absolute-value style sequence in it_802962E0 from assignment plus if-negate into a ternary expression using ip->xDD4_itemVar.tarucann.x10 directly.
Relabeled several GALE01 symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global it_* names to local @NNN labels for related .data string, .sdata strings, and .sdata2 float/double constants.
smart_moves: Promoted the unit only after both code and data reached matching/linkable status, as shown by configure.py switching ittarucann.c to Matching and the bot reporting new code and data matches.
Matched data by defining real C objects for known constants and the item state table instead of leaving them as external linker symbols.
Used local @NNN symbol labels for compiler-generated constants and strings, avoiding misleading global it_* names for data that appears local to the compilation unit.

Used expression-shape changes rather than semantic changes to fix codegen: the temporary assignment for 1.3089969f and ternary absolute-value pattern are small source rewrites targeted at register/order differences.
Kept a visible @todo near the float regswap-sensitive code, preserving context for why the source shape is non-obvious.
decomp_lessons: For item units, matching often requires the state table to live in the C file with exact callback ordering, motion-state IDs, NULL entries, and function-pointer casts.
When extern data is actually part of the target object, replacing externs with const definitions can unlock .data/.rodata/.sdata2 matches and allow the object to link.
Symbol-map cleanup is part of matching: constants emitted as local compiler labels should be represented as @NNN local symbols rather than named global it_* addresses.
Tiny C expression rewrites can resolve the last few bytes of a function match without changing runtime behavior.
For float/reg allocation mismatches, forcing an intermediate variable assignment can influence evaluation order/register use enough to complete a match.
A conditional operator can generate different code from an equivalent if block; in this PR, the ternary form completed the match for the absolute-value clamp path.
naming_conventions: Item-unit functions retain the existing mixed naming style: public logic callbacks such as it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned and local behavior callbacks such as itTarucann_UnkMotion7_Phys.
Known address-based data symbols use it_XXXXXXXX names when global, for example it_803B8610, it_803B8634, and it_803F63C0.
Compiler-local constants/strings in symbols.txt use @NNN local labels, for example @312, @313, @314, @188, and @350.
Inline helper naming follows the local pattern inline_itTarucann_SetRotationZ.
The state table symbol remains address-based as ItemStateTable it_803F63C0[] rather than receiving a descriptive gameplay name in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed `ip->xDD4_itemVar.tarucann.x8 = temp + 1.3089969f;` to `ip->xDD4_itemVar.tarucann.x8 = (temp_var = 1.3089969f) + temp;` to influence float evaluation/register behavior.
Changed an if-based absolute value into a ternary expression: `x10 < 0.0f ? -x10 : x10`.
Added explicit `(HSD_GObjEvent) itTarucann_UnkMotion7_Phys` casts in ItemStateTable entries where the callback type needed to match the table field.
Defined constants and tables in C to match .data, .rodata, and .sdata2 rather than relying on external declarations.
Relabeled emitted literals/constants from global symbols to local @ labels in symbols.txt to reflect compiler output.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 progress: matched code 54.54% (+0.02%, +652 bytes), linked code 31.30% (+0.17%, +6664 bytes), matched data 34.55% (+0.03%, +320 bytes), and linked data 28.92% (+0.03%, +336 bytes).
The bot listed 5 new matches for main/melee/it/items/ittarucann: .data +200 bytes, .rodata +48 bytes, .sdata2 +5 bytes, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned +2 bytes, and it_802962E0 +2 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that moved item state tables from linker symbols into C definitions.
Search for other uses of temporary float assignments to solve float regswap issues.
Search for ittarucann fake1 fake2 to see whether later cleanup removed or explained these matching helpers.
Compare other item units' ItemStateTable entries for HSD_GObjEvent callback casts.
Find PRs that relabeled global it_* constants to local @NNN symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
